info.name: Property Manager API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Property Manager platform.

**Setup:**
1. Set the `baseUrl` variable to your server (default: `http://localhost:3002`)
2. Run **Auth / Login** — the `authToken` variable is set automatically via the test script
3. All subsequent requests use `{{authToken}}` in the Bearer header automatically

request.description: Verify the server is running. No auth required.
Register a new user. Role can be `admin`, `tenant`, or `staff`.
Login and receive a JWT. The token is automatically saved to `{{authToken}}`.

No Authorization header needed. Reads the httpOnly `refreshToken` cookie set by login. Returns a new short-lived access token and rotates the cookie. Call this at app boot (page refresh) and whenever a 401 is received.
Clears the httpOnly refreshToken cookie. No body or Authorization header required. The client should also discard its in-memory access token.

Public. Sends a password-reset email if the address is registered. Always returns 200 to prevent user enumeration.

Check the `notification_log` table or the notifications log endpoint for the reset token in local dev.
Public. Consumes the one-time reset token and sets the new password. Token expires after 1 hour. Returns 400 if the token is invalid or expired.
Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user.

query.description: Filter by role: admin | tenant | staff

request.description: Admin only. List all users with optional role filter.
Users can update their own profile. Admins can update any user.
Admin only. `propertyType`: `single` | `multi` | `commercial`
Admin only. Only include fields you want to change.
Admin only. Will fail with 409 if the property still has units.
Admin only.
Admin only. `status`: `vacant` | `occupied` | `maintenance`
Admin and staff only.

Admin only. Links an already-existing user (role=tenant) to a tenant record. For the standard landlord workflow, use POST /invitations instead — that is the invite-based self-signup flow where the tenant sets their own password.
Admin only.

item.name: Invitations
item.description:
Landlord-driven tenant invite flow.

**Workflow:**
1. Admin sends an invite (POST /) — email and/or SMS delivered automatically.
2. Tenant clicks the link → validate token (GET /:token).
3. Tenant completes signup (POST /:token/accept) → JWT returned, tenant auto-logged in.

No user or tenant record exists until step 3.

request.description:
Admin/staff only. Creates a pending invitation and sends a signup link to the tenant via email and/or SMS.

At least one of `email` or `phone` is required. `unitId` is optional — if provided the tenant is automatically scoped to that unit after they sign up.

No user or tenant record is created at this point.

exec: console.log('Invitation token saved. Signup URL: ' + pm.variables.get('baseUrl').replace(':3000', ':5173') + '/accept-invite/' + json.token);

request.description: Admin/staff only. Returns all invitations (pending, accepted, expired) with property and unit context.

Public — no auth required. Validates the token and returns pre-fill data (name, email, property, unit) for the signup form.

Returns 404 if not found, 410 if already accepted or expired.

Public — no auth required. Tenant completes signup by providing their details and choosing a password.

On success: creates the user (role=tenant) and tenant record, marks the invitation accepted, and returns a JWT + user. The frontend uses this to auto-login the tenant.

The httpOnly refresh cookie is also set on the response — standard /auth/refresh flow works immediately after.

exec: console.log('Tenant signed up. authToken, userId, tenantId saved.');

request.description: Admin/staff only. Re-sends the signup link for a pending invitation. The existing token is reused — no new token is generated. Returns 409 if the invitation is already accepted.
Admin/staff only. Deletes a pending invitation. Returns 204 No Content. Returns 409 if the invitation has already been accepted.
Admin only. Automatically marks the unit as `occupied` and records the deposit charge in the ledger.
Admin only. Setting `status` to `terminated` or `expired` automatically marks the unit as `vacant`.
Terminates the lease and marks the unit as vacant.

Admin only. Income statement across your entire portfolio, or a single property/building.

Each property row includes:
- `totalCharged`   — total rent + fees billed
- `totalCollected` — total payments actually received
- `totalCredits`   — credits/adjustments issued
- `netIncome`      — collected minus credits (what you kept)
- `outstanding`    — billed but not yet paid
- `units[]`        — per-unit breakdown of the same fields

Filter by `fromDate`/`toDate` for a monthly or annual statement.
Returns the full append-only ledger history and running balance for a lease. Read-only audit trail.

query.description: Filter by unit (all charges ever billed to this unit)
true = only charges with no completed payment

request.description:
Returns charges with flexible filtering. Provide at least one of `unitId`, `tenantId`, `leaseId`, or `propertyId`.

Each row includes `unit_number`, `property_name`, and completed payment details (`payment_id`, `amount_paid`, `payment_date`, `payment_method`, `payment_status`, `stripe_payment_intent_id`) — all NULL when unpaid.

Tip: use `?unpaidOnly=true` to see what still needs collecting.
All outstanding charges for a unit with no completed payment. Use these charge IDs in POST /payments/stripe/payment-intent.
All charges linked to a specific tenant across all units/leases.
Returns a single rent_charge row.

Admin only. Creates a charge for a unit during an active lease. Because `leaseId` is provided, a `charge` ledger entry is also appended atomically — the tenant's running balance increases.

`chargeType`: `rent` | `late_fee` | `utility` | `other`

**Full flow:**
1. POST /charges → get `charge.id`
2. POST /payments/stripe/setup-intent (once per tenant bank account)
3. POST /payments/stripe/payment-intent `{ leaseId, chargeId }`
4. Stripe webhook → payment completed + ledger balance decreases

Admin only. Creates a standalone charge for a unit with no lease (utility bill, cleaning fee during vacancy, etc.). No ledger entry is created — the charge is a standalone billing item.

The charge response will have `ledgerEntry: null`. You can still collect payment via Stripe by passing `chargeId` in POST /payments/stripe/payment-intent (you'll need the unit's current active leaseId for that endpoint).

Admin only. Records a cash or check payment and appends to the ledger atomically. `paymentMethod`: `stripe_ach` | `stripe_card` | `check` | `cash` | `other`. Leave `chargeId` empty to record an unlinked payment.

Admin only. Creates a Stripe SetupIntent so the tenant can add a bank account (ACH). Returns a `clientSecret` the frontend passes to `stripe.collectBankAccountForSetup()`. Call once per new bank account — the resulting PaymentMethod ID can be reused for all future rent payments.

Admin only. Creates a Stripe PaymentIntent for the lease rent amount (or a specific charge if `chargeId` provided). Returns a `clientSecret` for frontend confirmation, plus a pending `paymentId` in our DB.

If `paymentMethodId` is provided (tenant already has a verified bank account), the intent is confirmed immediately server-side.

The ledger is only updated after Stripe confirms payment via webhook — never here.

body.raw:
{
  "unitId": "{{unitId}}",
  "category": "plumbing",
  "priority": "high",
  "title": "Leaking kitchen faucet",
  "description": "The kitchen faucet has been dripping constantly for two days."
}

request.description: Tenants and admins can submit. `category`: `plumbing` | `electric` | `hvac` | `appliance` | `structural` | `other`. `priority`: `low` | `medium` | `high` | `emergency`
Admin/staff: all fields. Tenants: may only set `status` to `cancelled` on their own open requests. `status`: `open` | `in_progress` | `completed` | `cancelled`
Returns all file attachments for the given request. Access-controlled — tenants see only their own requests' attachments.

formdata.description: Select a file to upload. Allowed types: JPEG, PNG, WEBP, GIF, PDF, MP4, MOV. Max size: 20 MB.

request.description: Uploads a file to Google Drive and records the attachment. Send as `multipart/form-data` with field name `file`. Allowed MIME types: `image/jpeg`, `image/png`, `image/webp`, `image/gif`, `application/pdf`, `video/mp4`, `video/quicktime`. Max 20 MB.
Deletes the file from Google Drive and removes the DB record. Admins/staff can delete any attachment. Tenants can only delete their own uploads. Returns 204 No Content.
Uploads metadata and triggers Google Drive upload. `category`: `lease` | `id` | `insurance` | `inspection` | `receipt` | `other`

query.description: rent_due | rent_overdue | late_fee_applied | lease_expiring | maintenance_update | payment_received | custom

request.description: Admin only. List all notification templates. Filter by channel or triggerEvent.

body.raw:
{ "name": "Rent Due Reminder", "channel": "email", "triggerEvent": "rent_due", "subject": "Rent due on {{due_date}} — {{property}} Unit {{unit}}", "bodyTemplate": "

Hi {{first_name}},

This is a reminder that your rent of {{amount}} is due on {{due_date}} for unit {{unit}} at {{property}}.

Please ensure payment is made on time to avoid late fees.

Thanks,
{{landlord_name}}

" }

request.description: Admin only. Supported {{placeholders}}: `{{tenant_name}}`, `{{first_name}}`, `{{due_date}}`, `{{amount}}`, `{{unit}}`, `{{property}}`, `{{landlord_name}}`, `{{lease_start}}`, `{{lease_end}}`, `{{status}}`, `{{description}}`
Admin only. Send a direct email — no template required. Provide `recipientId`, `subject`, `html`, and optional `text` fallback.

Admin only. Render a saved template with variable substitution and send. `variables` object replaces `{{placeholder}}` tokens in the template's subject and body. Works for both email and SMS channels — the correct deliver method is determined by the template's channel field.
Admin only. Send a direct SMS to a user — no template required. The user must have a `phone` number on file. Sends via Twilio and logs to notifications_log with channel='sms'.
Admin only. View all notification log entries. Filter by recipientId, channel, or status.
Admin only. View a single log entry including the fully rendered subject and body.

item.name: Conversations
item.description:
Two-way email conversations between landlord and tenants.

Each email thread is grouped by tenant. The landlord replies via `/messages` with `replyToEmail` set to the tenant's original message ID to thread correctly in the tenant's email client.

request.description: Admin only. Returns a summary of all email conversations grouped by tenant — one row per tenant showing the most recent message and unread count.
Admin only. Returns the full message thread for a specific tenant in chronological order. Each message has `direction` (inbound | outbound) and `messageId` (SES Message-ID for threading).

body.raw:
{ "tenantId": "{{tenantId}}", "subject": "Re: Your maintenance request", "html": "

Hi,

We have received your request and a technician will visit on Thursday between 10am–12pm.

Thanks

", "text": "Hi, we received your request and a technician will visit Thursday 10am-12pm. Thanks.", "replyToEmail": "" }

request.description:
Admin only. Sends an email reply to a tenant.

Set `replyToEmail` to the `messageId` of the tenant's inbound message to thread the reply in their email client (`In-Reply-To` / `References` headers are set automatically).

Leave `replyToEmail` empty to start a new thread.

header.description: Generated by Stripe — do not set manually in production

request.description:
Called by Stripe. Signature verification required — do not call manually in production. Use the Stripe CLI (`stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3002/api/v1/webhooks/stripe`) for local testing.

Handled events:
- `payment_intent.succeeded` → marks payment completed, appends ledger entry, sends receipt email
- `payment_intent.payment_failed` → marks payment failed
- `payment_intent.canceled` → marks payment failed

Returns 200 for all valid (even unhandled) events. Returns 400 only if the Stripe signature is invalid.

urlencoded.value: Hi, when is my rent due?

request.description: Called by Twilio when a tenant sends an inbound SMS. Validates the Twilio signature (when TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN is set) and logs incoming messages from known users.

header.description: Must match SES_WEBHOOK_SECRET in .env. Set the webhookSecret collection variable.

body.raw:
{ "messageId": "test-message-id-001", "fromEmail": "tenant@example.com", "fromName": "Test Tenant", "toEmail": "reply@lotlord.app", "subject": "Question about my lease", "text": "Hi, I had a question about my lease renewal date. Can you let me know when it expires?", "html": "

Hi, I had a question about my lease renewal date. Can you let me know when it expires?

", "inReplyTo": null, "references": [] }

request.description:
Called by the SES Lambda after it parses an inbound .eml from S3.

Authentication: `x-webhook-secret` header must match `SES_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. In local dev (no secret set), header is optional.

The server responds 200 immediately and processes the email asynchronously:
- Matches `fromEmail` to a known tenant
- Logs the message to the notification log (direction=inbound)
- Identifies or creates a conversation thread

**Tip:** Set `webhookSecret` collection variable to your `SES_WEBHOOK_SECRET` value.

item.name: SES Bounce / Complaint Webhook (SNS)
item.description:
SNS delivers two message types to this endpoint:
- `SubscriptionConfirmation` — auto-confirmed; only sent once after CDK deploy
- `Notification` — bounce or complaint from SES; marks affected email address(es) as bounced in DB

Authentication via `?secret=` query param (set by CDK in the SNS subscription URL).

request.description:
Simulates an SNS bounce notification. The server parses the inner `Message` JSON, extracts `bouncedRecipients[].emailAddress`, and marks those addresses as bounced in the users table.

Always returns 200 — SNS retries on any non-2xx.

Set `webhookSecret` collection variable to your `SES_WEBHOOK_SECRET` value.

Simulates an SNS SubscriptionConfirmation. The server validates the SubscribeURL is an `*.amazonaws.com` host (SSRF guard) and then GETs it to confirm the subscription. In production, CDK handles this automatically on deploy.
Admin only.
Admin only. Returns the full message history for an AI conversation.

item.name: Billing
item.description: Landlord subscription billing powered by Stripe. Landlords can subscribe, manage their subscription, and admins can view all landlord billing statuses.

request.description: Returns the current user's subscription status. Includes `plan`, `status` (active | trialing | past_due | canceled), `currentPeriodEnd`, and feature access flags.

Creates a Stripe Checkout Session for the landlord to subscribe. Returns a `url` to redirect the user to Stripe's hosted checkout page.

`priceId` is the Stripe Price ID for your subscription plan. Get it from the Stripe Dashboard.
Creates a Stripe Billing Portal session. Returns a `url` to redirect the landlord to Stripe's self-service portal where they can update payment methods, view invoices, and cancel their subscription.
Admin only. Returns all landlords with their Stripe subscription status, plan, and billing period details.

item.name: Analytics
item.description: Portfolio-level analytics and KPIs for the dashboard.

request.description:
Admin only. Returns KPIs across the portfolio:
- Occupancy rate and vacant unit count
- Total monthly rent billed vs collected
- Outstanding balance
- Open maintenance requests by priority
- Recent payment activity
- Expiring leases in the next 30/60/90 days

item.name: Audit Log
item.description: Immutable audit trail of all admin actions (creates, updates, deletes).

query.description: Filter by entity: user | property | unit | tenant | lease | charge | payment | maintenance | document | notification_template | invitation
Filter by action: create | update | delete

request.description: Admin only. Returns the audit log across the platform. Each entry includes `userId`, `action`, `entityType`, `entityId`, `oldValue`, `newValue`, and `createdAt`.
